preamble: The close back rounded vowel /u/ lowers to the close-mid back rounded vowel [o] through emphasis spreading in Moroccan Arabic and related contexts, where high back vowels lower in proximity to emphatic consonants. This process demonstrates vowel lowering as a phonetic consequence of emphatic consonant coarticulation.

examples.note: High back vowel lowers to mid-back position due to emphasis spreading from adjacent emphatic consonants (tˤ, dˤ, sˤ, zˤ, qˤ)
Emphasis spreading from emphatic ḍ causes lowering of preceding /u/ in Moroccan varieties
In Great Vowel Shift contexts, some /u/ shifted toward [o] position

related.label: Parallel emphasis-conditioned vowel lowering for front vowels
